name: zendesk-automation
description: Automate Zendesk tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): tickets, users, organizations, replies. Always search tools first for current schemas.
capabilities: List and search Zendesk tickets with pagination and sorting options
Create, update, and delete support tickets with full field control
Add public replies and internal notes to existing tickets
Search for and create Zendesk users with role assignment
Manage Zendesk organizations including creation and updates
Retrieve ticket details including comments and audit history
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 158 static analysis detections are false positives from Markdown documentation. The backtick-enclosed tool names (e.g., ZENDESK_LIST_ZENDESK_TICKETS) were incorrectly flagged as Ruby/shell commands and weak cryptography. This skill contains only instructional documentation with no executable code. The single hardcoded URL is the legitimate Rube MCP server endpoint required for setup.
valueStatement: Eliminate repetitive Zendesk tasks by automating ticket management, user lookup, and organization updates through natural language commands. Streamline your support workflow without leaving your development environment.
limitations: Requires active connection to Rube MCP service and authenticated Zendesk account
Closed tickets cannot be modified; must create follow-up tickets instead
Tag updates replace all existing tags unless merged manually
Rate limited to 400 requests per minute (varies by Zendesk plan)
Ticket deletion is permanent and irreversible

useCases.title: Support Triage Automation
useCases.description: Quickly search, filter, and categorize incoming tickets by priority, status, or assignee to optimize agent workflows.

useCases.title: Bulk Ticket Management
useCases.description: Create multiple tickets, update statuses, or add internal notes across tickets without using the Zendesk interface.

useCases.title: User and Organization Administration
useCases.description: Look up user details, create new accounts, and manage organization associations directly from development tools.

promptTemplates.title: List Recent High-Priority Tickets
promptTemplates.prompt: List all high-priority tickets from the last 7 days, sorted by newest first. Show me the subject, status, and requester for each.
promptTemplates.scenario: Morning triage review for support team leads

promptTemplates.title: Create Ticket with Assignment
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new Zendesk ticket with subject 'Login issue for customer account', priority 'high', and assign it to user John Doe (john@example.com). Set the status to 'open'.
promptTemplates.scenario: Escalating customer issues to specific agents

promptTemplates.title: Add Internal Note to Ticket
promptTemplates.prompt: Add an internal note to ticket #12345 with the text 'Customer confirmed they are using Chrome browser on Windows 11. Awaiting screenshots.'
promptTemplates.scenario: Documenting customer communications for team visibility

promptTemplates.title: Update Multiple Ticket Tags
promptTemplates.prompt: For ticket #67890, add the tags 'billing' and 'refund-request' while preserving existing tags. Update the status to 'pending'.
promptTemplates.scenario: Categorizing tickets for reporting and routing

bestPractices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to get the current tool schemas, as they may change
Use ZENDESK_SEARCH_ZENDESK_USERS to find user IDs before assigning tickets to agents
Set public=false when adding internal notes that should only be visible to support agents
Fetch current tags before updating to avoid losing existing tags
antiPatterns: Do not delete tickets unless absolutely necessary, as deletion is permanent and irreversible
Do not update closed tickets; create a follow-up ticket instead
Do not skip the connection verification step, as inactive connections will cause all operations to fail
Do not assume ticket IDs are sequential; always use the IDs returned by API responses

faq.question: Do I need a Zendesk API key?
faq.answer: No API key is required. This skill uses the Rube MCP service which handles OAuth authentication with Zendesk. You only need to authenticate through the Rube connection management flow.

faq.question: Can I work with tickets in bulk?
faq.answer: Yes, you can list up to 100 tickets per page and iterate through pages. However, each create/update/delete operation must be done individually. Be mindful of the 400 requests per minute rate limit.

faq.question: What happens when I update ticket tags?
faq.answer: Tag updates replace all existing tags on the ticket. To preserve existing tags, first fetch the current ticket details, merge the new tags with the existing ones, then update with the complete tag list.

faq.question: Can I reopen closed tickets?
faq.answer: No, closed tickets cannot be modified. If you need to continue working on a closed issue, create a follow-up ticket that references the original closed ticket number.

faq.question: What is the difference between public replies and internal notes?
faq.answer: Public replies (public=true) are sent to the customer via email and visible in the ticket thread. Internal notes (public=false) are only visible to agents and used for internal collaboration.

faq.question: Why does the skill require Rube MCP?
faq.answer: Rube MCP provides the integration layer with Composio's Zendesk toolkit. This service handles authentication, rate limiting, and provides a unified interface for Zendesk API operations.
